description_long: This is an electric guitar with a distinctive single-cutaway body shape, featuring a bold black and white bullseye pattern on the top surface. The body appears to be made of solid wood with a glossy finish, and the hardware, including the bridge, pickups, and control knobs, is metallic with a chrome or nickel appearance. The neck is dark, likely rosewood or ebony, with visible frets and inlays, and it extends to a headstock that is painted white and fitted with six tuning pegs in a 3+3 configuration. The guitar's design is reminiscent of the iconic Zakk Wylde Les Paul, known for its striking concentric circle motif. The instrument is equipped with two humbucker pickups and a three-way toggle switch, suggesting versatile tonal options. Overall, the guitar exudes a high-end, rock-oriented aesthetic suitable for stage performance or urban street music.
description: A solid-body electric guitar with a black and white bullseye pattern, chrome hardware, and a white headstock with six tuning pegs. It features two humbucker pickups and a glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bullseye pattern, chrome hardware, and the full face of the guitar body.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the guitar's thickness, the contour of the body, and the length of the neck.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the headstock, the neck, and the body outline, with tuning pegs visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other edge of the body and neck, with the headstock and tuning pegs in profile.
attribute_model: Les Paul Custom Zakk Wylde Bullseye
attribute_headstock_design: White, 3+3 tuning peg layout, likely with a logo on the front, classic Les Paul shape
